entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Farming system involving reduced tillage, permanent soil cover, and crop rotations to enhance soil fertility

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The primary crop studied in the farming systems of Southern Africa

entities.name: MAIZE GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced by maize, used as a measure of productivity

entities.name: RAIN-FED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Agricultural conditions where water is supplied solely by rainfall

entities.name: REDUCED TILLAGE
entities.description: A conservation agriculture practice of minimizing soil disturbance

entities.name: PERMANENT SOIL COVER
entities.description: A conservation agriculture practice of maintaining soil coverage

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: The practice of growing different crops in succession to improve soil health

entities.name: SOUTHERN AFRICA
entities.description: The geographical region where conservation agriculture was promoted for maize

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth

entities.name: TILLAGE
entities.description: The mechanical preparation of soil

entities.name: RESIDUE RETENTION
entities.description: The practice of leaving crop residues on the soil surface

entities.name: SOIL TEXTURE
entities.description: The relative proportion of sand, silt, and clay in the soil

entities.name: NITROGEN INPUT
entities.description: The application of nitrogen-based nutrients to the soil

entities.name: CLIMATE
entities.description: The long-term weather patterns affecting crop production

entities.name: STABILITY ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to measure the variability of crop yields

entities.name: HIGH INPUT USE
entities.description: The application of high levels of fertilizers and other agricultural inputs

entities.name: LOW RAINFALL AREAS
entities.description: Regions characterized by limited annual precipitation

entities.name: ANNUAL RAINFALL
entities.description: The total amount of precipitation received in a year

entities.name: META-ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical approach to combine and analyze data from multiple experiments

entities.name: MULCH COVER
entities.description: The use of organic or inorganic materials to cover the soil surface

entities.name: HIGH RAINFALL AREAS
entities.description: Regions characterized by abundant annual precipitation

entities.name: WATERLOGGING
entities.description: The saturation of soil with water, which restricts oxygen availability

entities.name: WELL-DRAINED SOILS
entities.description: Soils that allow water to move freely, preventing saturation

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A critical nutrient required for maize yield improvement

entities.name: SEMI-ARID AREAS
entities.description: Regions with low and irregular rainfall

entities.name: FERTILISER
entities.description: Chemical or organic substances added to soil to increase nutrient content

relationships.description: leads to lower yields in high rainfall areas
leads to lower yields in semi-arid areas without mulch
most important determinant in southern Africa
